sanskrit:
कर्मजं बुद्धियुक्ता हि फलं त्यक्त्वा मनीषिणः।
जन्मबन्धविनिर्मुक्ताः पदं गच्छन्त्यनामयम्॥
transliteration:
karma-jaṃ buddhi-yuktā hi phalaṃ tyaktvā manīṣiṇaḥ |
janma-bandha-vinirmuktāḥ padaṃ gacchanty anāmayam ||
literal_meaning: The wise, yoked through discriminating mind, having abandoned the fruit of action, freed from the bondage of birth, attain the state free from sorrow.
contextual_meaning: True psychological freedom is not the avoidance of action but the release of compulsive attachment to outcomes; when the ego’s ‘pay-off’ is relinquished, the cycle of repeated self-definitions (birth) dissolves and the mind rests in an equanimity untouched by gain or loss.
psychological_state: Post-attachment equanimity; the mind is no longer a shareholder in the ego’s profit-loss statement.
trigger_scenario: You just hit ‘submit’ on a job application and notice you’re obsessively checking email for praise or rejection—this verse invites you to drop the refresh-button and walk away free.
anchor_text: Do your best, let go of the outcome, and stay peacefully free.
